layers.name: Upstream Framework (nanobot)
layers.description: Core nanobot agent framework providing LLM agent loop, channels, providers, tools, config, and session management that Ava extends via monkey patches

layers.name: Sidecar Patch Layer (ava/patches)
layers.description: Monkey patch modules that intercept and extend nanobot framework behavior at startup, including schema, config, context, loop, tools, and channel modifications

layers.name: Sidecar Core (ava)
layers.description: Ava sidecar extensions including agent intelligence (memory, compressor, summarizer), custom tools (claude_code, vision, page_agent), storage, runtime lifecycle, and forks

layers.name: Console Backend API
layers.description: Aiohttp-based web console backend providing REST routes and services for chat, config, media, skills, gateway, and user management

layers.name: Console Frontend UI
layers.description: React + TypeScript + Vite single-page application with Tailwind CSS and Zustand state management for the web management console

layers.name: WhatsApp Bridge
layers.description: Node.js TypeScript bridge service connecting nanobot to WhatsApp via a WebSocket server

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest and shell-based test suite covering agent, channels, providers, patches, tools, console, config, and guardrails

layers.name: Specification & Documentation
layers.description: SpecAnchor three-tier spec system (global, module, task) plus project documentation, guides, skills definitions, and prompt templates

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Docker deployment, CI/CD pipeline, project-level config files (pyproject.toml, package.json), shell scripts, and git hooks

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand nanobot-ai's purpose: an ultra-lightweight personal AI agent supporting 10+ chat channels and multiple LLM providers. The key architectural insight is the sidecar pattern -- Ava extends nanobot without modifying upstream code, adding a Web Console, custom AI tools, SQLite storage, and enhanced memory. This separation is the central design principle that shapes everything in the tour ahead.

tour.title: Sidecar Entry Point
tour.description: The entry point for the entire application is 'python -m ava', which delegates to ava/launcher.py. The launcher discovers all patch modules under ava/patches/ in lexical order, applies them via a registry pattern, then hands control to the nanobot CLI. This two-step startup -- patch first, then run upstream -- is the mechanism that makes the sidecar architecture work without touching nanobot code.
tour.languageLesson: Python's __main__.py convention lets a package be executed with 'python -m package_name'. The launcher uses a registry pattern where each patch self-registers via a module-level call, and discovery happens by scanning the patches directory alphabetically.

tour.title: Upstream Framework Core
tour.description: Before understanding the patches, you need to see what they are patching. nanobot.py is the main facade providing Nanobot.from_config() and run() for single-shot agent execution. The agent loop in nanobot/agent/loop.py orchestrates message handling, tool dispatch, MCP connections, and session management. Together these form the upstream engine that Ava intercepts and extends.

tour.title: Message Bus and Channels
tour.description: The message bus defines InboundMessage and OutboundMessage dataclasses that flow between chat channels and the agent core. The channel manager discovers and initializes all enabled chat platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WeChat, etc.), routes outbound messages with retry logic, and manages channel lifecycles. This is the I/O layer that Ava's channel_patch and bus_patch will extend.

tour.title: Patch System Specification
tour.description: The global patch spec is the authoritative contract for how sidecar extensions work: zero upstream pollution, alphabetical patch ordering, self-registration via register_patch(), safety checks, and version compatibility rules. Understanding this document is essential before reading any individual patch, as it explains the naming conventions, testing requirements, and the new-patch checklist that every contributor must follow.

tour.title: Foundation Patches
tour.description: The first two patches (alphabetically) lay the groundwork for everything else. The schema patch replaces the upstream config schema with an Ava fork that adds fields for all supported channels, console, and token stats. The config patch then enhances config loading to support workspace-relative paths and Ava-specific defaults. These must run first because every subsequent patch depends on the extended configuration model.
tour.languageLesson: Monkey patching in Python works by replacing module-level references at runtime. The schema patch uses sys.modules replacement to swap an entire module, while later patches typically replace individual functions or methods on existing classes.

tour.title: Core Behavior Patches
tour.description: The loop patch is the most important behavioral extension, injecting token stats collection, memory consolidation, media handling, background tasks, and real-time observe events into the agent loop. The context patch enriches message building with personal memory injection and provider-specific sanitization. The tools patch registers Ava's custom AI tools (Claude Code, vision, image gen, sticker, memory). Together these three patches transform nanobot from a generic agent into Ava's personalized assistant.

tour.title: Infrastructure Patches
tour.description: The remaining patches handle infrastructure concerns. The storage patch replaces file-based session persistence with SQLite. The bus patch adds WebSocket broadcasting for real-time Console updates. The console patch co-launches the Web Console alongside the gateway. The channel patch adds batched send callbacks and streaming deltas. The skills patch enables runtime skill toggling via database. Each patch is self-contained, following the zero-upstream-pollution principle.

tour.title: Custom AI Tools
tour.description: Ava's custom tools are the user-facing capabilities that differentiate it from vanilla nanobot. The tools barrel module re-exports eight tool classes: ClaudeCode and Codex for programming, PageAgent for web browsing, ImageGen and Sticker for media creation, Vision for image understanding, Memory for explicit memory operations, and GatewayControl for runtime management. These are registered into the agent by the tools_patch from Step 7.

tour.title: Agent Intelligence Layer
tour.description: The agent extensions provide Ava's intelligence features beyond basic LLM interaction. The history compressor scores and selects relevant older turns to fit context windows. The history summarizer condenses full multi-message turns into compact summaries. The categorized memory system implements per-person memory with identity resolution. Background tasks use a write-many-read-once pattern for async operations. These modules are wired into the agent loop by the patches from Step 7.

tour.title: Web Console Backend
tour.description: The Console backend is a FastAPI application factory that wires up services for users, audit, config, files, gateway control, media, skills, chat, and token stats. It uses JWT-based authentication with role-based access control (the highest fan-in node in the codebase at 13 incoming edges). The Pydantic models define all API request/response schemas. The console is co-launched alongside the main gateway by the console_patch.

tour.title: Console Frontend and Bridge
tour.description: The React frontend defines role-guarded routes connecting pages for Chat, Dashboard, Config, Memory, and Browser. The WhatsApp bridge is a separate Node.js process that connects nanobot to WhatsApp via WebSocket. These represent the two non-Python subsystems in the project, each with their own build toolchain but integrated through the shared gateway and message bus.
tour.languageLesson: React Router with role-based ProtectedRoute guards implements client-side authorization. The pattern wraps route components in a guard that checks the user's role before rendering, redirecting unauthorized users to a login page.

tour.title: Deployment Infrastructure
tour.description: The Dockerfile builds a single image on Python 3.12 with Node.js 20 for the bridge and console UI, creating a non-root user and exposing gateway (18790) and console (6688) ports. Docker Compose defines three services: nanobot-gateway, nanobot-api, and an on-demand CLI, all sharing common build config with resource limits. The CI pipeline enforces a guardrails check that blocks unauthorized nanobot/ directory changes -- enforcing the sidecar rule from the code review level.
tour.languageLesson: The CI guardrails job is a key architectural enforcement mechanism: it automatically blocks PRs that modify nanobot/ files unless explicitly authorized, ensuring the zero-upstream-pollution principle is maintained through automation rather than just documentation.
